question: The coupled diagnostic harness reports microphysics_thompson = NOISY_ZERO. Is this a coupling bug or the physically correct INACTIVE_PHYSICAL answer?

evidence.init_case: real Gen2 d02, 20260521 18:00 UTC (dusk over the Canaries)

explanation: The d02 initial condition is sub-saturated everywhere (max RH 92.85%, 0 cells >=100%) and carries no pre-existing cloud or precipitation species. The WRF Thompson scheme correctly performs no microphysical conversion when there is no supersaturation and no condensate -- so every moisture-species delta is bit-exactly 0 in fp64. The harness NOISY_ZERO label is a classifier-threshold artifact: in the harness's default fp32 path a ~1e-14 K round-trip noise in the theta<->T conversion sits just above the absolute-zero activation floor while all moisture deltas remain exactly 0, which trips NOISY_ZERO instead of INACTIVE_PHYSICAL. The thompson_adapter IS correctly wired and called every step (operator_was_called=true, 36/36 opportunity steps); it would activate the instant a column saturates. Microphysical correctness was independently validated against the real WRF oracle on branch worker/opus/b1-thompson (proofs/b1/oracle_parity.md, water-closure PASS).

harness_operator_verdicts.microphysics_thompson: NOISY_ZERO -> INACTIVE_PHYSICAL on this sub-saturated case (see above)
